original_hypothesis.title: GaugeController.remove_gauge Always Reverts for Gauges with Active Weight Due to Type-0 Underflow
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In remove_gauge, gauge_types_[_gauge] is set to 0 BEFORE calling _remove_gauge_weight. Inside _remove_gauge_weight, gauge_type = gauge_types_[_gauge] - 1 evaluates to -1 (int128). _get_sum(-1) returns 0 because time_sum[-1] is uninitialized. The subsequent calculation new_sum = 0 - old_weight_bias causes a uint256 underflow revert in Solidity 0.8 whenever old_weight_bias > 0. This makes remove_gauge permanently non-functional for any gauge that has active votes.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The ordering of state mutation (zeroing gauge_types_ before calling _remove_gauge_weight) is demonstrably incorrect. The function is broken for its primary use case.

proof_of_signal.pattern: gauge_types_[_gauge] = 0 executed before _remove_gauge_weight(_gauge) which reads gauge_types_[_gauge] - 1

preconditions: Target gauge has any positive weight (votes allocated to it)
Governance calls remove_gauge without first calling remove_gauge_weight

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: State (gauge_types_) zeroed before internal function reads it; _remove_gauge_weight reads gauge_types_[_gauge]-1 = -1; _get_sum(-1) returns 0; new_sum = 0 - old_weight_bias underflows
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: remove_gauge is non-functional for active gauges — governance cannot remove malicious or deprecated gauges
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: gauge_types_ should be zeroed AFTER _remove_gauge_weight, or type should be passed as parameter

analysis_questions: Can governance work around this by calling remove_gauge_weight before remove_gauge?
Even with the workaround, does _remove_gauge_weight in remove_gauge corrupt storage by writing to type -1?
Are there any gauges in the system that cannot be removed at all?
validation_requirements: Attempt remove_gauge on a gauge with positive votes; confirm revert
Test workaround sequence: remove_gauge_weight then remove_gauge
Check storage writes to points_sum[-1] for corruption

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Governance deploys a gauge that turns malicious. Governance attempts to remove it via remove_gauge but the transaction always reverts due to underflow. The malicious gauge continues receiving emissions indefinitely.
prerequisites: Gauge has any positive vote weight

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Inability to remove compromised gauges means continued misdirection of CANTO emissions
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All veCANTO holders and liquidity providers affected by malicious gauge emissions
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Partial workaround exists via separate remove_gauge_weight call, but governance may not know this

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for remove_gauge on a gauge with active votes

validation_result.mechanism: state mutation ordering causes uint256 underflow in remove_gauge
validation_result.impact_summary: remove_gauge always reverts for gauges with active vote weight due to gauge_types_ being zeroed before _remove_gauge_weight reads it, making governance unable to remove active gauges.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The hypothesis is fully confirmed by tracing the code. In remove_gauge (GaugeController.sol:170), gauge_types_[_gauge] is set to 0 BEFORE _remove_gauge_weight(_gauge) is called on line 171. Inside _remove_gauge_weight (line 280), gauge_type = gauge_types_[_gauge] - 1 evaluates to 0 - 1 = -1 (int128). The call to _get_sum(-1) on line 285 checks time_sum[-1] which is 0 (uninitialized), so it returns 0. On line 290, new_sum = 0 - old_weight_bias causes a uint256 underflow revert in Solidity 0.8 whenever old_weight_bias > 0. This makes remove_gauge non-functional for any gauge that has received votes. Even if the workaround of calling remove_gauge_weight first is used, the subsequent remove_gauge call still writes to points_sum[-1] and changes_sum[-1], corrupting storage at negative mapping indices.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof_of_signal at GaugeController.sol lines 170-172 is accurate. Line 170 sets gauge_types_[_gauge] = 0, line 171 calls _remove_gauge_weight(_gauge), and line 280 inside _remove_gauge_weight reads gauge_types_[_gauge] - 1 which is now -1. The underflow at line 290 (new_sum = old_sum_bias - old_weight_bias where old_sum_bias=0 and old_weight_bias>0) is a definitive revert in Solidity 0.8.16 checked arithmetic.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: gauge_types_[_gauge] is set to 0 on line 170 BEFORE _remove_gauge_weight is called on line 171. This causes _remove_gauge_weight to read a type of -1, leading to underflow.
When called from remove_gauge, gauge_types_[_gauge] is already 0, so gauge_type = -1. _get_sum(-1) returns 0. old_weight_bias > 0 for active gauges. new_sum = 0 - old_weight_bias underflows uint256.
When gauge_type = -1, time_sum[-1] is 0 (uninitialized), so this function returns 0. This 0 value is used as old_sum_bias, causing the underflow in _remove_gauge_weight.

calling_sequence: 6. old_weight_bias = _get_weight(_gauge) returns positive value for active gauge (line 283)
8. new_sum = 0 - old_weight_bias REVERTS due to uint256 underflow (line 290)
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The root cause is the ordering on lines 170-171: gauge_types_[_gauge] is set to 0 before _remove_gauge_weight reads it. _remove_gauge_weight (line 280) computes gauge_type = gauge_types_[_gauge] - 1 = -1. _get_sum(-1) (line 285) checks time_sum[-1] which is 0, returns 0. Line 290 computes new_sum = 0 - old_weight_bias, which underflows for any gauge with votes.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment configuration affects this bug. The defect is in the code logic itself.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Governance deploys and adds a gauge. Users vote for it, giving it positive weight. If the gauge becomes malicious or needs deprecation, governance calls remove_gauge(). The transaction always reverts because of the uint256 underflow. The malicious gauge cannot be removed and continues receiving CANTO emissions indefinitely. The only workaround is to first call remove_gauge_weight() (public governance function) to zero the weight, then call remove_gauge(). However, even with this workaround, remove_gauge still writes to storage at type index -1 (points_sum[-1], changes_sum[-1]), causing silent storage corruption.
validation_result.impact_assessment: CRITICAL: Governance cannot remove any gauge that has active vote weight via the intended remove_gauge function. This creates a denial of service on gauge management. Compromised or deprecated gauges continue receiving emissions. While a partial workaround exists (calling remove_gauge_weight first), the remove_gauge function is fundamentally broken for its primary use case, and the workaround still causes storage corruption at negative type indices.
recommended_mitigations: Move gauge_types_[_gauge] = 0 to AFTER the _remove_gauge_weight call: _remove_gauge_weight(_gauge); gauge_types_[_gauge] = 0;
Alternative: Pass the gauge type as a parameter to _remove_gauge_weight instead of reading it from state: function _remove_gauge_weight(address _gauge, int128 gauge_type) internal
Add a test case for remove_gauge on a gauge with active votes to catch this regression
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy VotingEscrow and GaugeController. 2. Governance adds a type and a gauge. 3. A user locks CANTO in VotingEscrow and votes for the gauge via vote_for_gauge_weights with weight > 0. 4. Advance time past one week so weights are active. 5. Governance calls remove_gauge(gauge_address). 6. Observe the transaction reverts with arithmetic underflow. 7. Verify workaround: governance calls remove_gauge_weight(gauge_address) first, then remove_gauge(gauge_address). 8. Check storage corruption at points_sum[-1].
uncertainties: Whether the storage writes to negative mapping indices (type -1) in the workaround path cause exploitable state corruption in practice
additional_testing_needed: Test remove_gauge on gauge with active votes - confirm revert
Test workaround: remove_gauge_weight then remove_gauge - confirm success but check storage
Test if storage writes to points_sum[-1] affect any other valid type's storage slots
Test if users who voted for a removed gauge can reclaim their vote_user_power
